commit.message:
Fix heredoc delimiter quoting: apply quote-removal before matching closing line (#320)

The flash lexer stores heredoc delimiter words verbatim (e.g. `<<'EOF'`
→ `HereDoc("'EOF'")`), but bash's closing delimiter is the result of
quote removal on that word — just `EOF`. The dparser was comparing raw
closing-line tokens against the unstripped delimiter, so quoted heredocs
never closed.

## Changes

- **`src/dparser.rs`** — adds `strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes(delim)`
implementing POSIX quote-removal:
  - Single-quoted spans: `'FOO'` → `FOO`
- Double-quoted spans with inner backslash handling per POSIX (`$`, `` `
``, `"`, `\`, newline are special; others keep the backslash)
- Unquoted backslash escapes: `\X` → `X`; lone trailing `\` kept
literally
  - Mixed/partial quoting: `E'O'F` → `EOF`
- Applied at the `HereDoc`/`HereDocDash` match arm before pushing to the
heredocs queue

- **`src/command_acceptance.rs`** — adds
`test_here_documents_quoted_delimiter` covering all four quoting forms
(`'…'`, `"…"`, `\`, mixed) and the `<<-` dash variant

- **`src/dparser.rs` tests** — adds
`test_strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes` (unit) and per-form annotation
tests verifying `IsOpening`/`IsClosing` pairing

```bash
# Previously: heredoc never closed, will_bash_accept_buffer returned false
cat <<'EOF'
hello
EOF        # ← closing line didn't match "'EOF'" stored in queue
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Heredoc denim quoting</issue_title>
> <issue_description>I don't currently handle heredoc delim quoting.
From the bash reference:
> 
> 
> The format of here-documents is:
> 
> [n]<<[−]word
>         here-document
> delimiter
> The shell does not perform parameter and variable expansion, command
substitution, arithmetic expansion, or filename expansion on word.
> 
> If any part of word is quoted, the delimiter is the result of quote
removal on word, and the lines in the here-document are not expanded. If
word is unquoted, delimiter is word itself, and the here-document text
is treated similarly to a double-quoted string: all lines of the
here-document are subjected to parameter expansion, command
substitution, and arithmetic expansion, the character sequence \newline
is treated literally, and ‘\’ must be used to quote the characters ‘\’,
‘$’, and ‘`’; however, double quote characters have no special meaning.
> 
> 
> Write some test cases in dparser that use a quoted heredoc. Single
quotes, double quotes, and backlashes , and combinations. Add some
simple examples to the command_acceptance module.
> 
> Then see if the contents is appropriately parsed. And try and improve
the parser where required.</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

- Fixes HalFrgrd/flyline#319

commit.message:
feat: Add networking abstraction and core package enhancements

- toxnet: New high-level networking abstraction package for packet and stream transport
  - Implements PacketConn, Listener, Dial abstractions
  - Callback router for event-driven packet handling
  - Time provider for testable timeout/deadline management
  - Documentation (PACKET_NETWORKING.md, README.md)

- friend: Enhanced friend management with sharded state and persistence
  - FriendStore: Persistent friend state storage interface
  - Manager: Centralized friend lifecycle management
  - ShardedState: Sharded in-memory state for improved concurrency

- dht: Improved DHT peer discovery and storage
  - GossipBootstrap: Enhanced bootstrap with gossip protocol support
  - StorageCommon: Shared DHT storage utilities

- simulation: Network simulation for testing
  - PacketDeliverySim: Simulates packet delivery with loss/latency

- crypto/limits: Extract protocol constants
  - Constants: Centralized Tox protocol limits and constraints

- Update AUDIT.md and PLAN.md with ToxAV blocker analysis and pre-key cleanup completion

Cleanup: Remove test Tor/I2P keys and temporary Tor data directories

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix GitHub Pages 404s for reports and missing callgrind links in bench report (#207)

* Initial plan

* Fix GitHub Pages 404s and missing callgrind links in bench report

Three issues fixed in ci.yml:

1. Download ci-reports to docs/ (not docs/reports/) in deploy job.
   The artifact is uploaded with two paths (reports/ and bench_raw.json),
   so GitHub Actions preserves the reports/ prefix in the artifact.
   Downloading to docs/reports/ caused double-nesting at
   docs/reports/reports/... (404 for point_cloud_viewer.html and
   test-results.md). Downloading to docs/ maps reports/ correctly to
   docs/reports/.

2. Add family field to each callgrind matrix fixture so the
   family/id key format (e.g. direct-primitives/g4box-box-20x30x40-v1)
   matches what generate_bench_report.py uses when looking up callgrind
   links. Previously only the bare id was used as the key, causing
   callgrind_links.get(fixture_id) to always return None.

3. Update callgrind_links JSON key from bare id to family/id format.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/wdconinc/G4OCCT/sessions/29cfa731-76d4-4fcc-9fde-1e231f4f8941

Implement multiplayer visibility and attacking for new players

- Create RemoteTank entity to render other players with health bars and names

- Update GameScene to:

  - Handle player join/leave events immediately
  - Sync remote player positions, rotations, health
  - Render all bullets from server state
  - Send player inputs to server each frame
  - Display kill/respawn notifications
- Add vitest to server devDependencies (was missing)
- Fix type imports for verbatimModuleSyntax

New players are now immediately visible to all connected players and can be
attacked without any protection period or visibility delay.

Closes #2

commit.message:
security: fix CodeQL suppression comment rule IDs

Previous comments used incorrect IDs (js/network-data-written-to-file,
js/file-data-to-outbound-request) which CodeQL did not recognise.
Correct IDs are:
  js/http-to-file-access      — Network data written to file
  js/file-access-to-http      — File data in outbound network request

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: fix TOCTOU race conditions and suppress intentional data-flow alerts

Race conditions (js/file-system-race-condition):
- install.service: replace fs.access() + conditional writeFile() with atomic
  writeFile({ flag: 'wx' }) + catch EEXIST for NeoForge and Fabric version
  manifest writes — eliminates the check-then-use window
- modpack-api.service: replace fs.stat() + fs.readFile() with a single
  FileHandle (open → stat → readFile on the same handle) so the file cannot
  be deleted between the existence check and the read

False positives (js/network-data-written-to-file, js/file-data-to-outbound-request):
- install.service: suppress writeFile alerts for pack manifest/versionManifest
  writes — a modpack launcher intentionally persists fetched pack data
- modpack-api.service: suppress writeFile alert for logo image cache write
- launch.service: suppress fetch body alert for user-triggered log upload
  to paste.myftb.de

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: exclude build artifacts from CodeQL scan, remove unneeded build step

CodeQL analyses TypeScript source directly and does not need a compiled
build. Running the build before analysis caused out/ and dist/ to be
included in the scan, producing false positives from bundled/minified
output. Remove the build step and add paths-ignore for out/, dist/, and
node_modules/ to keep the scan scoped to src/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: fix CodeQL warnings across install, java, API, and router services

- install.service: add isPathWithinDir() guard to reject manifest task.to
  paths that escape the instance directory via ../ traversal (js/path-injection)
- modpack-api.service: escape prop before inserting into new RegExp() to
  prevent regex injection (js/regex-injection)
- java.service: replace dynamic new RegExp() with a static Record<17|21,RegExp>
  lookup — required is always a number but CodeQL flags it (js/regex-injection)
- router.ts: add explicit https:/http: protocol check before shell.openExternal
  to silence open-redirect alert (js/open-url-redirection)
- launch.service: strip newlines from packName before writing desktop entry
  fields as a defense-in-depth measure
- tests: add install-path-guard.test.ts (7 cases for isPathWithinDir) and
  extend java-version.test.ts with two cross-version rejection cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: block all major version bumps in Dependabot

A vite 7→8 PR broke the build because electron-vite doesn't support
Vite 8 yet. The previous config only blocked majors for electron-vite
and @vitejs/plugin-react but not vite itself.

Replace the per-package ignore list with a global rule that blocks all
major bumps. Major upgrades always require manual migration work anyway
and should not be opened automatically.

commit.message:
B2: Apply quick-win balance optimizations across 5 config files (#47)

Conservative manual balance pass over externalized JSON configs:

- Woodcutting patterns: Fix difficulty-to-reward monotonicity (e.g.,
  Perfect Straight 3.0x→15.0x, Four of a Kind 10.0x→3.5x, reduce
  Eight of a Kind from 55.0x→30.0x, increase straights/sets)
- Crafting durability: Reduce grant 500→300 (10:1→6:1 ratio)
- Scouting: Rebalance asymmetry from (-15%,+30%) to (-20%,+25%)
- Mining: Narrow stamina cost from (50,350) to (80,200)
- Rest: Reduce overpowered recovery (health_cost_stamina 900-1200→500-700,
  stamina_cost_health 600-800→400-550)
- Combat: Reduce health_start_damage from (1000,1300) to (700,950)

fix: prevent CopilotProvider.Close() from acting on never-started client

Add 'started' flag to CopilotProvider so Close() only calls client.Stop()
when ensureStarted() has actually been called. Previously, startErr's nil
zero-value made Close() assume startup succeeded even when Start() was
never invoked — a correctness bug that could break with future SDK changes.

Also add COPILOT_CLI_PATH test safeguard to root_ai_test.go tests to
prevent accidental SDK process spawning during test execution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): add platform-specific benchmark thresholds for Apple Silicon

macOS ARM VMs amplify Go binary-layout non-determinism: adding code to
a package changes function alignment and instruction cache behavior,
causing 15-39% timing swings on unchanged functions.

- Linux: keep 15% threshold (stable runners)
- macOS: use 40% threshold (binary-layout variance on Apple Silicon)
- Same-runner comparison retained (eliminates cross-machine variance)
- Memory threshold stays at 10% on both platforms

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement Withings provider — weight, BP, sleep, activity

New package: @biosync-io/provider-withings

OAuth2 flow:
- Authorization: account.withings.com/oauth2_user/authorize2
- Token exchange/refresh: wbsapi.withings.net/v2/oauth2
- Scopes: user.metrics, user.activity
- Withings uses 'action=requesttoken' in token body (non-standard)

Data sync (3 endpoints):

1. Body Measurements (measure?action=getmeas):
   - Weight (kg), Body Fat (%), Blood Pressure (systolic+diastolic),
     Heart Rate (from BP device), SpO2, Temperature
   - Handles Withings value encoding: realValue = value * 10^unit

2. Sleep (v2/sleep?action=getsummary):
   - Full sleep structure: deep/light/REM/awake durations
   - Sleep score, efficiency, latency, wakeup count
   - Heart rate avg/min/max during sleep
   - Respiratory rate (rr_average)

3. Activity (v2/measure?action=getactivity):
   - Steps, distance, calories
   - Active minutes (moderate + intense)

14 metric types supported: weight, body_fat, blood_pressure,
heart_rate, resting_heart_rate, sleep, sleep_score, blood_oxygen,
temperature, respiratory_rate, steps, calories, distance,
active_minutes

Registered in both API and Worker index.ts.

Build verification:
- Provider: ✅ builds (14.72 KB)
- API: ✅ 0 TS errors, builds (461 KB)
- Worker: ✅ builds
- Web: ✅ 0 TS errors
- Tests: ✅ 51/51 passed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): use same-runner benchmark comparison to eliminate cross-machine variance

PR benchmarks now run both main and PR code on the same runner instance,
eliminating 30-50% variance from cross-machine Apple Silicon comparisons.

Stored baselines on main push preserved for historical tracking.

commit.message:
Add JS module layer for web-v2 prototype (7 ES modules)

Creates prototype/web-v2/js/ with 7 ES modules implementing the full
game loop for the web-v2 Aconcagua: Stone Sentinel prototype.

- state.js: createInitialGameState, updateRunState, assertStateShape, clamp
- engine.js: faithful port of v1 EP/BT/delta pipeline; loadEngineData,
  initRun, resolveTurn, calculateEP, calculateBT, getActionModifier,
  getPerceivedState, getAvailableActions, route accessors, RNG helpers
- characters.js: loadCharacters (with accent/stars augmentation),
  getCharacterById, selectRandomCharacter
- scenarios.js: loadScenarios, getScenarioById, buildRandomScenario
- i18n.js: full ES/EN bilingual catalog, setLanguage, getLang, t()
- ui.js: DOM rendering — screen management, hero/character/briefing/
  game/debrief screens, action buttons, status panel, event log,
  perception card, position list, theme cycling
- app.js: main controller — init, navigation, language/theme/difficulty
  with localStorage persistence, character+scenario selection, full
  turn-based game loop, debrief flow, window.app facade

All 21 existing v1 tests continue to pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add fuzzy case/accent-insensitive name matching for OCR member identification

Replace all 6 exact-substring name comparisons with _names_match() which:
- Normalizes to lowercase and strips accents (é→e, ô→o, etc.)
- Checks substring containment first
- Falls back to SequenceMatcher fuzzy ratio (≥80% threshold)

This correctly matches 'MARYLINE LAMARCHE' to 'Marylin Lamarche' (97% similar)
and 'HÉLOÏSE CÔTÉ' to 'Heloise Cote'.

Applies to both scan session review and single-receipt review paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(web-v2/css): address code review feedback

- tokens.css: improve --text-hero clamp() with absolute offset; add
  --nav-height / --topbar-height layout tokens; add --shadow-pulse-*
  tokens so animation keyframes reference variables instead of raw hex
- base.css: mark hanging-punctuation as progressive enhancement
- components.css: replace hardcoded #0B1117 with var(--bg); replace
  per-theme nav rgba overrides with color-mix(in srgb, var(--bg) 85%,
  transparent) so all 3 themes are handled automatically
- game.css: replace per-theme topbar rgba overrides with color-mix on
  var(--bg); replace hardcoded top/height magic numbers with
  var(--nav-height) / var(--topbar-height); replace raw rgba()
  backgrounds on .action-btn.action-summit / .action-special with
  color-mix(in srgb, var(--surface2) 90%, var(--summit|--moss))
- screens.css: replace all hardcoded 56px padding-top with
  var(--nav-height); add descriptive comments on clip-path polygons
- responsive.css: replace 56px/48px magic numbers with tokens; split
  select out of the touch-target rule (height-only, no min-width)
- animations.css: use --shadow-pulse-summit / --shadow-pulse-alert
  tokens in pulse-glow keyframes

When creating a new budget year, the inline sub-budget formset is now
pre-populated with the previous year's active sub-budgets (names, amounts,
trace codes, sort order). The treasurer can modify, delete, or add

shown instead.

commit.message:
feat(async): add write-ahead log and dynamic message limits

- Implement WriteAheadLog (wal.go) for crash recovery with entry serialization
- Add WAL integration to MessageStorage: EnableWAL, DisableWAL, RecoverFromWAL
- Enable log-on-write for store/delete operations with optional checkpointing
- Replay uncommitted WAL entries after crash to restore message state
- Update dynamic limits tests with capacity-based message limit scaling
- Improve TestStorageCapacityLimits to verify error messages
- Update ROADMAP.md to mark dynamic per-recipient limits as implemented

commit.message:
Improve prose readability per code review feedback

Address three readability issues identified in review:
- BON paragraph: split into two logical sentences by function
  (educational/credentialing vs. enforcement/regulatory)
- Licensure levels paragraph: break RN sentence at semicolon into
  two distinct sentences for easier parsing
- Malpractice categories paragraph: restructure to reduce dense
  em-dashed nesting; move category names to sentence subjects

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Convert bullet-point lists to prose in Chapter 2

Convert all standalone bullet-point lists throughout Chapter 2 into
flowing prose paragraphs to improve readability for nursing students.
Sections converted include:

- BON powers list (§2.1.1)
- Licensure levels by NPA (§2.1.2)
- Disciplinary sanctions spectrum (§2.1.3)
- Assignment vs. supervision definitions (§2.2.1)
- Non-delegable nursing activities (§2.2.3)
- Common malpractice categories (§2.3.2)
- HIPAA patient rights (§2.4.3)
- Boundary spectrum continuum (§2.6.1)
- Boundary violation categories (§2.6.2)
- License renewal requirements (§2.7.2)
- Nursing specialty certifications (§2.7.2)
- NLC practice contexts (§2.7.3)
- License discipline grounds (§2.7.4)
- Decision-making capacity elements (§2.8.2)
- Non-maleficence requirements (§2.8.4)
- Code of Ethics in practice example (§2.9.3)
- MORAL model case application (§2.10.4)
- Individual moral resilience strategies (§2.11.4)
- Institutional moral resilience strategies (§2.11.4)
- Ethics committee membership (§2.12.1)
- Ethics consultation triggers (§2.12.2)
- SDOH clinical examples (§2.13.2)
- Racialized health disparity examples (§2.13.3)

All clinical facts preserved. Tables, blockquotes, sidebars, learning
objectives, key terms, and NCLEX question answer choices unchanged.
Bullet count reduced from ~109 to 0 standalone bullets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(async): implement erasure-coded redundant storage with dynamic recipient limits

- Implement Reed-Solomon erasure coding (3+2 shards) across k=5 storage nodes
  for 99.9% message survival with 2-of-5 failure tolerance
- Add ErasureCodingConfig, ErasureEncoder, ErasureStorage, and EncodedShard
  for thread-safe shard management and reconstruction
- Add ErasureShardEnvelope for secure network transmission of shards
- Integrate erasure coding into AsyncClient with SetErasureCodingEnabled()
- Implement storeWithErasureCoding() and storeShardOnNode() for distributed storage
- Add SetErasureCodingEnabled(), IsErasureCodingEnabled(), GetErasureStorageStats()
  for client-side erasure coding control
- Create async/erasure_test.go with 20+ test cases covering encode/decode,
  partial reconstruction, concurrency, and benchmarks
- Implement dynamic per-recipient message limits in MessageStorage based on
  available capacity to prevent loss for popular users
- Add DynamicLimitConfig with CalculateDynamicRecipientLimit() function
- Add MaxPerRecipient and UtilizationPercent to StorageStats
- Update async tests to handle both simple redundancy and erasure coding errors
- Add helper function containsStorageNodeError() for flexible error checking
- Update ROADMAP.md with session 2026-03-22 implementation details
- Add go.mod/go.sum dependencies for erasure coding library (klauspost/reedsolomon)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Google Health Connect and Apple Health to providers list

Now showing 8 supported providers:
- Fitbit, Garmin, WHOOP, Strava (cloud API — OAuth)
- Withings, Polar (cloud API — OAuth, planned)
- Google Health Connect (Android on-device, requires companion app)
- Apple Health (iOS on-device, requires companion app)

Google Health Connect and Apple Health work differently from cloud
APIs — they aggregate data on-device from Samsung Health, Apple
Watch, Fitbit app, and 100+ other sources. Integration requires
a companion mobile app that reads local data and pushes to VitaSync.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: show 'Coming Soon' for unconfigured providers instead of config details

Users shouldn't see backend infrastructure details. Changed from
'Not Available — Platform admin needs to add API credentials' to
clean 'Coming Soon — integration not yet enabled on this instance'.

The Connect flow is simple when enabled:
1. User clicks '🔗 Connect {Provider}'
2. Redirects to provider (Fitbit/WHOOP/etc.) for permission
3. Provider redirects back to VitaSync
4. Connection established — syncing starts automatically

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: vitasync
repository.description: vitasync

commit.message:
fix: clarify provider config is platform-level, not per-user

Changed 'Not Configured — Add credentials to .env' to
'Not Available — Platform admin needs to add API credentials'.

Provider API keys (Fitbit Client ID, Garmin Consumer Key, etc.)
are platform-level credentials configured once by the operator.
All users then connect through OAuth. The old '.env' messaging
was confusing — made it sound like every user needs their own.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
